channel.title: Pocket Realms
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Corona
Unnamed 18
item.description: Terrain; Cloudpost, Glimmerpost, Trenchpost L^2 Planar Nexus 5 / Lsn Sliver Hive s Cryptic Spires n Glimmervoid n^2 Mana Confluence n^3 Echoes of Eternity n^4 Hive; Thrumming Hivepool triangular(n) Riptide Island sqrt(n) Crawlspace s Non-Legendary Slivers n Animals; Faeries Pests Worms Eldrazi Sapients; Kin - Insect, Germ, Gremlin, Varmint

item.title: Hytropo
item.description: The realm is named Hytrop(i/a/o). (Hi-trope-(ee/ah/oh))Each denizen calls it either Hytropi, Hytropa, or Hytropo; based upon the gender they identify with. -i for non-binary or fluid, -a for feminine, and -o for masculine.-e (ey) is another suffix, used for neutral gender. -u (oo) is another suffix used for unknown genders or to be formal. Attaching a -y to a suffix turns it plural.Hytrope and Hytropu are uncommon forms of the realm’s name, but possible to encounter.Within the language of the denizens of the realm, all nouns used these suffixes. To use a car as an example, if no one was driving it, the word would end in -e, as it is a simple object without a will or soul, but if a man was driving it, it would end in -o and a woman driving it, -a.Names utilize this method as well, however, it is based on the person who owns the name to choose a suffix, unlike with objects.Terrain; Cloudpost, Glimmerpost, Trenchpost L^2 Planar Nexus 4 / Lsn Sliver Hive s Raffine's Tower n Contaminated Landscape 2n Glimmervoid n^1 Mana Confluence n^2 Echoes of Eternity n^3 Hive; Thrumming Hivepool triangular(n) Riptide Island sqrt(n) Crawlspace s Non-Legendary Slivers n Animals; Gremlins Varmints Pests Worms Chromium Eldrazi Sapients; Splinter - Halfdane Kin - Faerie, Insect, Germ

item.title: Komas
item.description: Terrain; Cloudpost, Glimmerpost, Trenchpost L^2 Planar Nexus 1 / Lsn Sliver Hive s Wastes n Blasted Landscape 2n Ash Barrens 4n Glimmervoid n^-2 Mana Confluence n^-1 Echoes of Eternity n^0 Hive; Thrumming Hivepool triangular(n) Riptide Island sqrt(n) Crawlspace s Non-Legendary Slivers n Animals; Faeries Insects Germs Gremlins Varmints Worms Wurmcoil Engine Coiled Tinviper Boulderborn Dragon Eldrazi Sapients; Echo - Eidolon of Philosophy Splinter - Scion of Ugin Kin - Pest

item.title: War Web
item.description:
During the Fomori-Eldrazi War, the Fomori of the Multiverse travelled across the chaos wall frequently. To facilitate the supply lines for this war, they built staging grounds into the chaos wall, spaces between the finite infinity between the multiverse and the edge.
These pockets stabilized the chaos, concentrated it into mana, and then concentrated it into matter. A self perpetuating Engine of expansion that served as staging grounds, relay stations, and recharging stations. Each one aligned with different colors of mana to serve as perfect 'batteries' for different machines and purposes.
The Fomori had many war machines, but with the war against the Eldrazi and Ixalan both going simultaneously, they implemented an unusual force to safeguard these locations from the near constant invasions of the Eldrazi. They used a peculiar organism they found within both the edge and the multiverse, Slivers.
Making artifacts to control their hiveminds, they were able to have the Slivers reliably safeguard the planar portals and eternity columns that were built into the center of these pockets, allowing themselves, their ships, and their war machines to pass through uncontested, while aggressively fighting and adapting to the Eldrazi menace. A strategy not unlike the methods employed by Volrath to guard the Legacy, but on a more grand scale.The Fomori did not come into these pockets entirely devoid of other forms of life. Bringing in the slivers and coming in themselves, they had various hitchhikers, ancestors of the flora and fauna that can be found in the pockets today. Though, as they were not intentionally seeded with life nor designed to host life other than Slivers, major forms of life are few and not particularly diverse.
Since the Fomori disappeared, the pockets expanded, life continued to adapt, and the concentrations of mana warped the landscape. As they expanded, they weakened the wall in specific nebulous points, which has facilitated the crossing between the two occasionally. Such as Tezzeret entering into the Edge.
Various unintended side effects of these pockets have become prominent over the millennia, leylines from the Edge slowly grows Moxite along them, while leylines from the Multiverse slowly grows Etherium along them. They act as a sort of 'self-repairing' 'natural' wire that conducts the mana into the central 'engine'. Overtime, the denizens of the realms have mined and harvested the materials, which has resulted in a higher concentration in the center of the realms, where the leylines converge and feed into the 'engine'.
The souls of the Slivers, along with the various modifications they made to their biology, were designed to use the leylines to feed into the 'engine'. The souls of everything else, was not. The dead have their souls shredded, mere remnants at best remain, while the rest travels along the leylines. The edges, emotions, memories, non-sliver form/identity, all snag along the leylines, forming and growing crystals of mana along them. Unlike the veins/wires of metal that grow from the outside inwards, these crystals grow from the inside outwards.Due to the constant invasions of Eldrazi and the hyper territorial Slivers, major settlements have never appeared on any of the realms. There are small pockets of society on many of the realms, but that is just about all there is in nations.
Every realm has independently developed their own forms of sentient life, each though, is an odd mirror of one another, perhaps due to the innate interconnectivity each of the realms possess through the 'engine's connected planar portals and eternity columns. Every realm has beastial animalfolk known simply as the 'Kin' and Aetherborn, who are born from the quelling of the blind eternities around the realms. Some realms have Echoes, gestalt souls of the dead; Remnants, machines left by the Fomori; and/or Splinters, individualist descended from the Sliver hives.
Oddly, Eldrazi incursions have become less frequent in the past decade or so. Similarly, Sliver attacks against Remnants travelling through the Hivelands have become more frequent.Despite each realm being objectively flat, a half-sphere of land and atmosphere, there is a visual illusion, perhaps induced by magic, perhaps some sort of gravitational distortion, that causes the realm to visually curve at roughly a rate of 0.009 degrees per kilometer to those on them.(For the purposes of anyone who looks into patterns, each integer is capable of being multiplied by 10 kilometers, starting where the prior ring ended and being that many km thick.)
(s is the number of legendary slivers in WUBRG there are, x is the number of legendary slivers in the color/hive, n is the number of unique arts for the legendary slivers in WUBRG (minus 1 for legion (crayon) and plus 1 for queen (duelmasters, L is the number of locus there are)))The more colors a realm is aligned with, the less stable the realm is, the further out reality needs to be stabilized, the more mana needs to be concentrated, and the more nanites are required to process and manufacture matter.The bottom of the multiverse lies a single realm, that of no color.
Komas - A realm devoid of colors of mana, unique for its mechanical Slivers. The first strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess only a single color of mana.
Lumos - A realm of white mana
Souse - A realm of blue mana
Nocturne - A realm of black mana
Ignis - A realm of red mana
Sett - A realm of green mana The second strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess a pair of colors of mana.
Unnamed 1 - A realm of wu mana
Ioxis - A realm of wb mana
Unnamed 2 - A realm of wr mana
Iethol - A realm of wg mana
Noxou - A realm of ub mana
Zyxkal - A realm of ur mana
Unnamed 3 - A realm of ug mana
Montresor - A realm of br mana
Unnamed 4 - A realm of bg mana
Unnamed 5 - A realm of rg mana The third strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess three colors of mana.
Hytropo - A realm of wub
Unnamed 6 - A realm of wur
Unnamed 7 - A realm of wug
Unnamed 8 - A realm of wbr
Unnamed 9 - A realm of wbg
Unnamed 10 - A realm of wrg
Unnamed 11 - A realm of ubr
Unnamed 12 - A realm of ubg
Unnamed 13 - A realm of urg
Kithestra - A realm of brg The fourth strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities lack a single color of mana.
Unnamed 14 - A realm of wubr
Unnamed 15 - A realm of wubg
Unnamed 16 - A realm of wurg
Unnamed 17 - A realm of wbrg
Unnamed 18 - A realm of ubrg The top of the multiverse lies a singular realm who's identity is all encompassing.
Corona - A realm of wubrg

item.title: Engine
item.description: The Planar Portal that enables travel to the multiverse and other realms.The Eternity Column that enables travel to the exoverse and the other realms.It coordinates the leylines from both realities towards the center, funnels souls into itself, it facilitates the stabilization of the chaos wall, the expansion of the realms, and the drive of the sliver hives to protect the center of the realm.

item.title: Kin
item.description:
The Kin are chimeric humanoids that take on the forms of specific beings that have survived upon the realms. As the realms expanded, they too became larger, until they have all become the size of a human, on average.Their cousins, who did not gain sapience, are smaller, cat sized at their largest, and in many cases, significantly smaller than that. The ones that did gain sapience have taken a more humanoid, or standing upright, appearance if their animalistic form does not already have one.Colorless - Pest Salamander/Frog-like creatures, often distinguished by being colorful, voracious, and being covered in spines, teeth, and claws. They have colored patterns on their bodies, one of the colors of which are associated with the core color of the realm. An example, for a realm where black is the core color of mana, they could have a purple or black color to their pattern, on top of potentially other random colors. Colorless is grey and WUBRG is a metallic gold. Their patterns often have three or four colors, but only one of which is guaranteed to be associated with the realm's core.

Blech, Loafing Pest by Ilse Gort
White - Faerie Diminutive humanoids with legs that end in small points, they are fond of tricks, mischief, thievery, jokes, and being helpful. Their wings are ten fabric-like ribbons of light that extend from their backs. They have small, smooth, curved horns. They have eight eyes, similar to a spider. Their skin is striped and shiny, similar to a wasp, though rather than yellow, they have human skin tones. Finally their hair is a metallic color, depending on the core color of the realm. Gold for white, Silver for blue, Lead for black, Iron for red, and Copper for green, etherium and moxite for colorless and WUBRG.
Blue - Insect Six limbed, four arms and two legs for sapients, have large compound eyes across the sides of their heads. They have hair along their bodies, most notably as a sort of main. They are a sort of species of ant-like beetle.

Chitin Gravestalker by Slawomir Maniak
Red - Gremlin They are six-limbed, vaguely anteater- or aardvark-like creatures that feed on aether, dismantling artifacts to get it. Their saliva is corrosive, allowing them to breach through metal hulls to reach their food. Their young are called grubs.

Gimbal, Gremlin Prodigy by Fajareka Setiawan
Black - Germ They are, generally speaking, microscopic organisms. However, they are responsible for the chimeric nature of the sapients, be it they are a part of it or not. The sapients are large colonial organisms of germs, looking like they are made of goo with the 'glow' color of the realm's core color. Yellow for White, Light Blue for Blue, Purple for Black, Bright Red for Red, Pale Green for Green, and Rainbow/Iridescent/Oilslick for WUBRG.

Irregular Cohort by Steve Argyle
Green - Varmint Like a cross between a reptile, skunk, and armadillo, they strange creatures that can eat just about anything.

item.title: Pasted image 20260412224516
Pasted image 20260412224029
Pasted image 20260412223937
Pasted image 20260412223235
Aetherborn
item.description: Aetherborn also known as 'Dreamers' amongst the realmic denizens, awaken at the edge of a realm.They are all considered to be gifted, capable of extending their lives by draining the souls of others or consuming the mana crystals, etherium, and moxite.They are capable of travelling between the realms by 'jumping off the edge' of a realm. Though a few are capable of doing so from any point on a realm, in a manner similar to planeswalking.

item.title: Echo
item.description: The gestalt souls of the dead that have formed physical bodies. Their natures vary significantly between the realms, both in personality and number.The one thing that is consistent, relatively, is their appearance. They take the shape of a Fomori with a featureless face, their shadows are like a Nyxborn's, their hair flames composed of mana (the colors of the realm), and their flesh a smooth black stone with veins of etherium within.

item.title: Remnant
item.description: Machines left by the Fomori, they have built up their own numbers, as time has progressed.One modification they typically give to themselves is an eternity engine, which they mine moxite to fuel.

item.title: Splinter
item.description: Splinters are odd humanoids, highly social, yet oddly solitary. They are descendants of Slivers that 'evolved individualism' as best they can figure, around the time the Fomori disappeared. Overtime, they have lost a majority of their 'sliveriness', they barely a hivemind, they are barely shapeshifters, and they are humanoid in form. They often regard the sliver hive in a similar manner to a human and a chimpanzee, not the same and not the same level of sentience/sapience generally.On the hivemind point, they have a thought-weft. They are able to telepathically communicate with one another and share emotions, but not share their thoughts, memories, or experiences. It also lends them a general sense of the direction and distance of the nearest sliver, which being no longer in the hive, they avoid.On the shapeshifter point, what little they can shapeshift depends drastically upon the realm they inhabit.On the humanoid point, beyond that, they look off. Their bodies are covered in chitinous plates, their eyes are more akin to gemstones that are the same color of the realm's colors mixed together, they have "hair" that is more like the squirming tentacles of a jellyfish or polyp than true hair, and they have cloaks of leathery flesh that is oddly similar to tanned human skin. They do not have ears or mouths that are visible, rather they seem to have plates around such structures. Their humanoid appearance is assumed to be a subconcious adaptation to put the other sapients at ease, the cloak of flesh to mimic clothes. Compared to normal slivers, these adaptations were made over the course of millenia rather than moments.

item.title: Hivelands
item.description: The center of each realm, with the exception of Corona, the sliver hives control a swath of the central area of the realm.These areas are constantly patrolled, protected by, and inhabited by Slivers. To such a degree that they are consistently considered to be inhospitable to any other form of faunal life. This central territory is considered to be 'the Hivelands'. Outside of those lands are considered to be habitable lands, assuming one is careful and safe.

item.title: Slivers
item.description:
Slivers have three methods of reproduction, one from the multiverse, one from the exoverse, and one unique to these pocket realms.
The first, unique to this plane, is autogenesis. The liquid metal pool in the heart of every hive that surrounds the Engine, periodically spawns thrum. The number spawned has increased over the course of centuries and millennia, initially having been one at 'midnight'.

Thrumming Hivepool by Rob ReyThe second, the multiversal method, is parthenogenesis. Slivers are capable of laying eggs via asexual reproduction. This can only be done while the individual's cortisol levels are low to non-existent, otherwise known as the individual feeling safe and secure. There are, of course, exceptions or modifications to this if the hiveform is specialized for reproduction in some capacity. Three notable examples being Sliver Queen, The First Sliver, and Hatchery Sliver. The queen can simply do this regardless of state and hatchery overdrives this process.

Hatchery Sliver by Svetlin VelinovThe third, the exoversal method, is oviparity. Specifically, utilizing externally fertilized parasitic ovipositors. Basically, xenomorphing people. Stabbing them with a specific tendrils that can extend from their beak-like 'faces' and implanting an embryo. It uses the genetics of the host to determine the color alignment and future adaptation (if any), technically the external fertilization is this process of utilizing the host's dna. If you are, at the time of being implanted, classified as a blue black being, then the sliver who emerges would either be colorless or blue black. The host will be unconscious for a day, due to adjacently injected toxins that quells the immune system and causes the host to believe they simply fell asleep rather than having been injected with an embryo, then it will develop over the course of ten days in the host, then spend five days after violently bursting from the host's torso to mature fully into a Thrum or fully adapted Sliver.

Syphon Sliver by Johann Bodin
